name: Web Search API
purpose: Executes high-speed, lightweight HTTP searches to return structured snippets, URLs, and real-time knowledge.

safety,cost,resources.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #2 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. Tavily completely offloads RAG synthesis to the cloud, guaranteeing absolute isolation from the macOS host while returning LLM-ready snippets.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #3 mandates a Dockerized provider. SearXNG runs inside an OrbStack container to provide a secure execution boundary while aggregating search results without third-party tracking.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 forbids paid Cloud APIs. With Safety as the secondary priority, SearXNG is Dockerized to ensure free local metasearch capabilities without exposing the bare-metal macOS host.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 forbids paid Cloud APIs. With Resources prioritized over Safety, SearXNG is executed bare-metal to completely bypass OrbStack virtualization overhead on the constrained 16GB RAM machine.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #3 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. Tavily ensures zero local compute is consumed for search aggregation and synthesis, preserving unified memory.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #2 mandates highly optimized native execution. SearXNG runs bare-metal to deliver parallel search aggregation with absolute minimal CPU/RAM impact.

safety,cost,resources.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #2 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. Tavily offloads execution, ensuring no malformed search data can exploit the Windows host.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #3 mandates a Dockerized provider. SearXNG leverages WSL2's highly efficient containerization to isolate the metasearch engine from the Windows filesystem.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 demands a free, locally hosted solution. Safety #2 dictates Dockerization, making WSL2-hosted SearXNG the optimal balance of zero financial expenditure and secure containment.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 demands a free solution. Resources #2 dictates bypassing WSL2 overhead where possible. SearXNG is executed bare-metal for maximum speed on a constrained Windows machine.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #3 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. This eliminates all local processing overhead, keeping the Windows host highly responsive.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #2 mandates highly optimized native execution. SearXNG is run bare-metal to maximize efficiency for local search aggregation.

safety,cost,resources.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #2 (and Tier <= 2) mandates a Cloud API. Exa's neural search and 'Highlights' extraction isolates the monolith while cutting local LLM token processing time by up to 50%.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #3 mandates a Dockerized provider. SearXNG provides containerized isolation for local metasearch, keeping the 24/7 monolith secure from rogue search responses.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 forbids paid APIs. SearXNG provides unlimited, free search capabilities, while Dockerization satisfies the Safety #2 requirement for the background monolith.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 forbids paid APIs. With Resources #2 over Safety, SearXNG is executed bare-metal to eliminate Docker network overhead while maintaining high resilience for 24/7 background tasks.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #3 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. Exa preserves the 32GB+ monolith's CPU/RAM entirely for VictoriaLogs and PostgreSQL vector indexing.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #2 strictly mandates optimized native execution. SearXNG runs bare-metal to ensure the background search daemon consumes minimal continuous resources.

safety,cost,resources.why: Safety #1 forbids bare-metal. Because this is Tier > 2, Cloud APIs are strictly forbidden. The rules therefore mandate a Dockerized deployment. SearXNG securely contains search operations in isolated containers on the blast-zone.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 forbids bare-metal. Because this is Tier > 2, Cloud APIs are strictly forbidden. The rules therefore mandate a Dockerized deployment. SearXNG securely contains search operations in isolated containers on the blast-zone.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 demands a free local solution. Safety #2 mandates containerization. SearXNG's self-hosted architecture is deployed inside Docker to securely contain the blast radius of heavy search aggregation.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 demands a free solution. Resources #2 dictates bypassing container overhead. SearXNG running natively bare-metal maximizes raw search throughput directly on the blast-zone CPU.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 demands minimal host impact. Because Tier > 2 strictly forbids Cloud APIs, we must bypass heavy Docker VMs and fall back to highly optimized native binaries. SearXNG runs bare-metal to maximize efficiency.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 demands minimal host impact. Because Tier > 2 strictly forbids Cloud APIs, we must bypass heavy Docker VMs and fall back to highly optimized native binaries. SearXNG runs bare-metal to maximize efficiency.
